seoTitle: How to Edit Desktop and Mobile Separately in Squarespace
metaDescription: Edit your desktop and mobile layouts separately in Squarespace to make sure your site looks and works great on every device.
intro: Desktop and mobile visitors interact with your site differently, so a layout that works well on one screen size doesn't always translate to the other. This guide explains how to edit desktop and mobile versions separately in Squarespace so both experiences feel intentional. It's a quick process that matters for anyone running a business portfolio or eCommerce site.

steps.heading: Access the editing mode
steps.body: Open the design editor in Squarespace where you can toggle between desktop and mobile views.

steps.heading: Understand the differences between devices
steps.body: Consider how layout, spacing, and content priorities differ between desktop and mobile before making changes.

steps.heading: Make key design adjustments
steps.body: Adjust elements individually for each device so your site looks intentional rather than just automatically resized.

steps.heading: Optimize for user experience
steps.body: Focus on making sure important content and calls to action are easy to find on both desktop and mobile.

steps.heading: Avoid common mistakes
steps.body: Watch for inconsistencies in branding or missing content that can happen when desktop and mobile versions aren't reviewed together.

faq.question: Why would I need to edit desktop and mobile separately?
faq.answer: User experience varies significantly between devices, so editing them separately helps ensure visitors have a good experience regardless of how they access your site.

faq.question: Does editing one version affect the other?
faq.answer: Squarespace lets you adjust desktop and mobile layouts independently, so changes to one don't automatically overwrite the other.

faq.question: Is this feature suitable for beginners?
faq.answer: Yes, the tutorial is designed to be approachable for beginners while still offering value for professionals looking to maximize Squarespace's flexibility.

faq.question: What mistakes should I watch out for?
faq.answer: Common mistakes include inconsistent branding or content that displays well on one device but gets cut off or misaligned on the other.
